2605.01341.title: ABox Abduction for Inconsistent Knowledge Bases under Repair Semantics
2605.01341.paper_summary: Given a knowledge base (KB) with a non-entailed fact, the ABox abduction problem asks for possible extensions of the KB that would entail this fact. This problem has many applications, ranging from diagnosis to explainability and repair. ABox abduction has been well-investigated for consistent KBs and classical semantics, but little is known for the case of inconsistent KBs, which can be caused by erroneous data. In this paper we define suitable notions of abduction in this setting and propose criteria that guide abduction towards "useful" hypotheses. To regain meaningful reasoning in the presence of inconsistencies, we use well-established repair semantics. We provide a comprehensive landscape of the complexity of ABox abduction under repair semantics, treating different variants of the abduction problem for the light-weight description logics DL-Lite and EL_bot.
2605.01341.paper_summary_zh: 給定一個包含非推導事實的知識庫 (KB)，ABox 反推問題要求尋找可能的 KB 擴展，以推導出該事實。這個問題有許多應用，從診斷到解釋性和修復。ABox 反推在一致的 KB 和經典語義下已經得到了充分的研究，但對於不一致的 KB 情況，因為錯誤數據而導致的不一致性，卻知之甚少。在本文中，我們定義了在這種情境下合適的反推概念，並提出指導反推朝向「有用」假設的標準。為了在存在不一致的情況下恢復有意義的推理，我們使用了成熟的修復語義。我們提供了在修復語義下 ABox 反推的複雜性全面概述，處理輕量級描述邏輯 DL-Lite 和 EL_bot 的反推問題的不同變體。
2605.01341.author: Anselm Haak et.al.
2605.01341.authors: Anselm Haak, Patrick Koopmann, Yasir Mahmood, Anni-Yasmin Turhan
